lever: de-delegate the directed case (was a full fnx->nx conversion, 1.5x-10x slower than nx depending on cycle depth) with an in-process port of nx's HEURISTIC SPFA _inner_bellman_ford (recent_update/pred_edge early negative-cycle detection) + nx's exact cycle-reconstruction stack walk, on a native to_dict_of_dicts snapshot. Returned cycle is byte-identical to nx.

parity.coverage: directed gnp with negative weights; cycle list AND the no-cycle NetworkXError path byte-exact to nx

headline: find_negative_cycle directed: de-delegated, ~1.9-4.8x self-speedup, byte-exact. Old delegation was 1.5x-10x slower than nx (conversion tax, worst when nx's heuristic finds the cycle early); new is 0.77x-2.37x vs nx (data-dependent: nx's lazy early-exit vs our upfront snapshot). Eliminates the worst-case 10x-slower delegation. Pure Python, no rebuild.
note: residual vs-nx is data-dependent — when the heuristic finds the cycle in few edges, nx's lazy G._adj beats our O(V+E) to_dict_of_dicts snapshot; when the cycle is deep, we win. Net big improvement over the prior delegation in all measured cases.
